data.title: WARRIOR MET COAL, INC. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Warrior Met Coal, Inc., a leading producer and exporter of premium steelmaking coal, has released its 2025 10-K report, detailing the company's financial and operational performance over the past year. The report highlights significant achievements in production, strategic initiatives, and the challenges faced in a volatile global market.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $1,310.0 million, reflecting a decrease of 15% from the previous year primarily due to a decrease in the average net selling price per metric ton of steelmaking coal.

Operating Income: $45.7 million, representing 3.5% of Total Revenues, down from 16.7% in the prior year due to lower sales prices and increased costs.

Net Income: $57.0 million, or 4.4% of Total Revenues, a significant decrease from the previous year's $250.6 million, impacted by lower average selling prices and increased interest expenses.

Diluted EPS: $1.08 per share, reflecting the decrease in Net Income compared to the prior year.

Cost of Sales: $982.4 million, or 75.0% of Total Revenues, decreased slightly from the previous year, driven by lower average cash cost of sales per metric ton due to the sales mix of Blue Creek coal.

Business Highlights

Production Volume: In 2025, Warrior Met Coal produced a record high of 9.3 million metric tons of steelmaking coal from its mines, including Mine No. 7, Mine No. 4, and the newly operational Blue Creek mine.

New Production Launch: The Blue Creek mine commenced longwall operations in October 2025, eight months ahead of schedule, significantly increasing production capacity and contributing to lower cash costs.

Geographical Performance: For the year ended December 31, 2025, Warrior Met Coal's sales geographic customer mix was 48% in Asia, 37% in Europe, 14% in South America, and 1% in the U.S., reflecting a strategic expansion into Asian markets.

Sales Units: The Blue Creek mine alone produced approximately 1.8 million metric tons in 2025, with expectations to produce between 4.1 to 4.4 million metric tons in 2026.

Future Outlook: With the anticipated addition of a fourth continuous miner unit, Blue Creek's nameplate production capacity is expected to increase to approximately 6.4 million metric tons per year, enhancing Warrior's position in the global cost curve.

Environmental Initiatives: Warrior Met Coal achieved a 37% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 GHG emissions from baseline year levels and a 34% water recycling rate in 2025, demonstrating progress towards its 2030 emissions reduction targets.

Operational Efficiency: The implementation of innovative technologies at Blue Creek has increased its nameplate production capacity by 25%, further improving Warrior's cost structure and profitability.

Strategic Resource Expansion: The finalization of federal coal leases with the Bureau of Land Management provides access to approximately 48 million metric tons of additional reserves, supporting long-term production visibility and growth.

Logistics Advantage: Warrior Met Coal's proximity to the Port of Mobile, Alabama, and its flexible logistics network provide a significant cost advantage in serving international markets, particularly in the Atlantic Basin.

Safety and Training: In 2025, Warrior Met Coal invested over $4.0 million in safety initiatives, achieving a total incidence rate of 1.96, which is significantly lower than the national average for underground coal mines.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: The company commenced longwall operations at the Blue Creek mine eight months ahead of schedule, significantly increasing production capacity and reducing costs. It finalized two federal coal leases, adding approximately 48 million metric tons of reserves, which supports strategic growth initiatives. The company also increased the nameplate production capacity of the Blue Creek mine by 25% due to innovative technologies and best practices.

Capital Management: The company maintained a strong balance sheet with total liquidity of $483.9 million, consisting of cash, short-term investments, and availability under the Amended ABL Facility. It paid $17.8 million in regular quarterly dividends and repurchased 500,000 shares for approximately $10.6 million under its stock repurchase program. The company also amended its ABL Facility to increase commitments and extend the maturity date. Additionally, it issued $350 million in senior secured notes due 2028 and repurchased a portion of these notes through a tender offer.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue investing in the final construction of Blue Creek, with capital expenditures expected to range from $155 million to $215 million in 2026. It aims to optimize its capital structure to improve returns to stockholders while allowing flexibility for strategic growth opportunities. The company will also focus on maintaining liquidity and managing debt, with potential future restricted payments in the form of special dividends or stock repurchases, subject to market conditions and board discretion.

Challenges and Risks

Challenges and Risks: The company faces significant risks related to global economic conditions, including the impacts of global pandemics, wars, and inflation, which may adversely affect business operations and financial performance. The ongoing Russia-Ukraine war and the recent Israel-Hamas conflict, along with trade disruptions in regions like the Persian Gulf, pose additional risks to the global steelmaking coal market, potentially affecting prices and demand.

Operational challenges include the potential for delays or issues in completing the development and ramp-up of the Blue Creek project, which could impact long-term growth. The company also faces risks from work stoppages and labor shortages, particularly given the high percentage of union-represented employees, which could lead to increased labor costs and operational disruptions.

The company is heavily reliant on steelmaking coal sales, which exposes it to market risks such as price volatility and demand fluctuations. The lack of diversification in its business model could adversely affect financial stability if steelmaking coal prices decline significantly.

Regulatory risks are substantial, with extensive federal and state environmental, health, and safety laws imposing significant costs. Future regulations could increase these costs or limit production capabilities. The company is also responsible for medical and disability benefits for black lung disease, with potential changes in claims or collateral requirements impacting financial results.

Management acknowledges the challenges posed by global economic volatility and geopolitical tensions, which could affect steelmaking coal demand and pricing. The company is focused on cost control and operational efficiencies to mitigate these risks but recognizes that these measures may not fully offset adverse economic conditions.

The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in steelmaking coal prices and foreign currency exchange rates, which could impact financial results. The reliance on a single rail carrier for transportation increases vulnerability to disruptions, although efforts are underway to test alternative logistics routes to mitigate this risk.

SEC Filing: WARRIOR MET COAL, INC. [ HCC ] - 10-K - Feb. 12, 2026

data.title: ROLLINS INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Rollins Inc., a leading provider of pest control services, has released its 2025 10-K report, showcasing robust financial and operational performance. The report highlights significant growth across various service offerings, strategic initiatives aimed at long-term sustainability, and the challenges the company faces in the evolving market landscape.

Financial Highlights

Rollins Inc. reported impressive financial results for the year ended December 31, 2025:

  • Revenues: $3,761.05 million, an increase of 11.0% from 2024, driven by strong customer demand across all major service offerings.
  • Gross Profit: $1,984.04 million, representing a gross profit margin of 52.8%, a slight improvement from 52.7% in 2024.
  • Operating Income: $726.07 million, reflecting a 10.5% increase over the prior year, with an operating margin of 19.3%.
  • Net Income: $526.71 million, a 12.9% increase compared to 2024, supported by strong operating results and effective cost management.
  • Net Income Per Share: $1.09, up from $0.96 in 2024, indicating a 13.5% growth in earnings per share.

Business Highlights

Rollins Inc. demonstrated strong business performance across various segments and geographies:

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported strong growth across its service offerings, with residential pest control revenue increasing by approximately 10%, commercial pest control revenue by 11%, and termite and ancillary services by 14%. Organic revenue growth was 5% in residential, 8% in commercial, and 10% in termite and ancillary services.
  • Geographical Performance: The company's foreign operations accounted for approximately 7% of total revenues for the years ended December 31, 2025, and 2024. Revenue growth was consistent throughout the year, although there was a noted decrease in volumes in the fourth quarter due to less favorable weather conditions.
  • Operational Efficiency: The company achieved a gross margin improvement of 10 basis points to 52.8% in 2025, compared to 52.7% in 2024. This was due to leverage across several cost categories, including materials and supplies and insurance and claims, despite higher fleet costs.
  • Customer Loyalty: The company maintained effective sales and service staffing levels, which helped capitalize on continued demand, resulting in a 6.9% growth in organic revenues compared to 2024.
  • People First Initiative: The company focused on developing its workforce by hiring and onboarding the right people into the right roles and introduced The Co-Lab to develop servant leadership skills among managers.
  • Future Outlook: For 2026, the company anticipates 7% to 8% organic revenue growth, supported by the underlying health of core pest control markets and a strategic approach to acquisitions. The company also expects ongoing modernization efforts and a culture of continuous improvement to support an improving margin profile.

Strategic Initiatives

Rollins Inc. has undertaken several strategic initiatives to ensure long-term growth and sustainability:

  • Strategic Pillars: The company is focused on continuous improvement across four strategic pillars: people first, customer loyalty, growth mindset, and operational efficiency. In 2025, significant strides were made in these areas, including the development of talent through initiatives like The Co-Lab, which enhances leadership skills.
  • Acquisitions: The company completed 26 transactions, including 22 acquisitions and 4 franchise buybacks, to drive inorganic growth both domestically and internationally.
  • Capital Management: Rollins issued $500 million in 2035 Senior Notes to repay outstanding borrowings and for general corporate purposes. The company also established a $1 billion commercial paper program to support various corporate needs, including acquisitions. In 2025, Rollins repurchased 3,478,260 shares of common stock for approximately $200 million and paid $327.9 million in cash dividends. The company maintains a $1 billion revolving credit facility, with no outstanding borrowings as of December 31, 2025.
  • Future Outlook: For 2026, Rollins anticipates 7% to 8% organic revenue growth, supported by strong core pest control markets and a disciplined acquisition strategy. The company plans to continue its modernization efforts and maintain a balanced capital allocation strategy, leveraging compounding operating cash flow and a strong balance sheet. Rollins also expects to realize an effective tax rate of 24.5% to 25% in 2026 and anticipates interest expenses of approximately $30 million associated with its 2035 Senior Notes and commercial paper program.

Challenges and Risks

Rollins Inc. faces several challenges and risks that could impact its future performance:

  • Cybersecurity: The company has implemented comprehensive cybersecurity measures, including the appointment of a new Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) with over 20 years of experience. Despite these efforts, the company acknowledges the potential risk of cybersecurity incidents that could impact the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of company data. The Audit Committee oversees cybersecurity risk management, and significant incidents are reported to the Executive Leadership Team for assessment.
  • Economic Challenges: The company faces several economic challenges, including inflation, changing interest rates, tariffs, trade disputes, and supply chain issues, which may adversely affect future performance. The company has implemented strategies to mitigate these impacts, but the effectiveness of these strategies remains uncertain. Additionally, the company is increasing its reliance on AI technologies, which introduces new risks that could materially affect the business.
  • Market Risks: The company is subject to interest rate risk through its borrowings under the revolving credit facility and commercial paper program. A one percent increase in interest rates is not expected to have a material effect on results. The company also faces foreign exchange rate risks, but these are not expected to materially impact future operations.

SEC Filing: ROLLINS INC [ ROL ] - 10-K - Feb. 12, 2026

data.title: JETBLUE AIRWAYS CORP SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
JetBlue Airways Corp, a major American low-cost airline, has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, providing a comprehensive overview of its financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The report highlights the company's efforts to navigate a competitive industry while focusing on customer satisfaction, operational efficiency, and strategic growth.

Financial Highlights

Total Operating Revenues: $9,062 million, reflecting a decrease of 2.3% compared to 2024, primarily due to softening demand.

Operating Loss: $368 million, an improvement from the $684 million loss in 2024, driven by cost reductions and lower fuel expenses.

Net Loss: $602 million, compared to a net loss of $795 million in 2024, with improvements attributed to lower fuel costs and benefits from strategic initiatives.

Loss Per Common Share: $1.66, compared to a loss per share of $2.30 in 2024, indicating a reduction in losses per share.

Business Highlights

JetBlue Experience: JetBlue offers a distinctive flying experience with competitive fares, focusing on customer satisfaction from booking to arrival. The airline provides three experiences: Core, EvenMore®, and Mint®, each with unique features and amenities.

Geographical Performance: As of December 31, 2025, JetBlue served 112 destinations across the U.S., Caribbean, Latin America, Canada, and Europe. The capacity distribution showed a focus on Caribbean & Latin America (36.5%), Florida (25.4%), and Transcontinental routes (26.0%).

Sales Units: JetBlue's available seat miles (ASMs) decreased by 1.6% in 2025 compared to 2024, reflecting a strategic reduction in capacity in response to demand fluctuations.

New Product Launches: In 2024, JetBlue announced JetForward, a strategic framework focusing on reliable service, an east coast leisure network, valued products, and financial security. The EvenMore® product was enhanced with additional amenities, and a premium credit card was launched.

New Production Launches: JetBlue opened its first airport lounge, BlueHouse, at JFK Terminal 5 in December 2025, with plans to open another at Boston Logan International Airport in 2026.

Future Outlook: JetBlue plans to launch a domestic first-class experience across its non-Mint® fleet starting in 2026, with completion expected by the end of 2027. The airline also aims to adopt Amazon's Leo satellite broadband network for improved onboard Wi-Fi by 2027.

Operational Adjustments: In 2025, JetBlue retired its Embraer E190 fleet, transitioning to an all-Airbus fleet to enhance cost efficiency and customer focus. The company also faced challenges with grounded aircraft due to engine inspections mandated by the FAA.

Partnerships: JetBlue announced a collaboration with United Airlines, Blue Sky, to enhance customer options and loyalty program benefits, with full implementation expected by 2026.

Customer Loyalty Program: The TrueBlue® loyalty program was expanded with new co-branded credit cards and partnerships, offering more opportunities for customers to earn and redeem points.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: JetBlue's strategic initiatives in 2025 focused on the JetForward plan, which aims to deliver reliable and caring service, build the best East Coast leisure network, offer valuable products and perks, and secure a financial future. Key actions included the introduction of Blue Sky, a collaboration with United Airlines, and the expansion of the Mint® service. The company also rebranded JetBlue Travel Products to Paisly, LLC, marking a strategic milestone in its evolution into a full-service, tech-enabled managed travel services company.

Capital Management: JetBlue maintained a strong focus on capital management by repaying $461 million on outstanding debt and finance lease obligations in 2025. The company also managed its liquidity with $2.5 billion in cash, cash equivalents, and investment securities, and a $600 million line of credit with Citibank. Additionally, JetBlue entered into definitive agreements to sell its remaining Embraer E190 fleet as part of its fleet transition plan, generating a net gain of $32 million. The company also issued $52 million in common stock related to its crewmember stock purchase plan.

Future Outlook: Looking ahead, JetBlue plans to continue its JetForward initiatives, focusing on cost savings and operational efficiencies through AI and data science technology. The company expects to maintain a healthy liquidity position and meet its long-term liquidity needs through projected cash from operations, available lines of credit, and debt financing. JetBlue also plans to adopt Amazon's Leo satellite broadband network for enhanced onboard Wi-Fi and launch domestic first class by 2026. The company anticipates further expansion in Europe with new seasonal transatlantic routes and aims to complete its fleet transition to an all-Airbus fleet by 2027.

Challenges and Risks

Competitive Risks: JetBlue faces significant competitive risks in the extremely competitive airline industry, characterized by low profit margins and high fixed costs. The company competes with larger airlines with greater financial resources and faces competition from surface transportation and technological alternatives like virtual meetings. Industry consolidation and regulatory changes could further alter competition dynamics. Additionally, JetBlue's long-term fleet commitments may hinder its ability to adopt new technologies quickly, potentially increasing maintenance costs and reducing revenues.

Operational Risks: Operational risks include the potential failure to execute the JetForward strategic plan, which could impact reputation, business, and financial condition. The plan's success depends on assumptions about customer demand, fuel costs, and other factors, which may not hold true. JetBlue's operations are also highly dependent on fuel availability and price, with significant volatility impacting financial results. The aging fleet will lead to increased maintenance costs, and as the workforce ages, salaries and benefits costs are expected to rise.

International Risks: JetBlue's international operations expose it to risks such as compliance with U.S. and foreign regulations, political and economic instability, and currency fluctuations. The company's high aircraft utilization rate makes it vulnerable to delays and cancellations, which could harm profitability and reputation. The concentration of operations in the New York metropolitan area poses risks related to congestion, competition, and potential reductions in demand.

Cybersecurity and Information Security Risks: Cybersecurity and information security risks are significant, with potential legal claims and reputational harm if there is a disruption to IT systems or a breach of confidential information. The reliance on automated systems and third-party providers for IT infrastructure presents additional risks.

Data Privacy and Security Compliance: Data privacy and security compliance requirements are increasing, potentially leading to higher costs and operational disruptions. Non-compliance could result in legal actions and financial penalties.

Human Capital Risks: Human capital risks include challenges in attracting and retaining qualified personnel, with potential increases in labor costs due to unionization and workforce aging. The company's reputation and financial results could be harmed by accidents or incidents involving aircraft, and the strong JetBlue brand could be damaged by adverse publicity or social media activity.

Management's Discussion and Analysis: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by the competitive airline industry and the need to adapt to technological advancements. The JetForward strategic plan aims to enhance service and financial stability, but its success depends on various assumptions that may not materialize. The company is focused on managing fuel price volatility through hedging strategies, though these do not fully protect against price fluctuations.

Market Risks: JetBlue is exposed to market risks such as fuel price volatility and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which could adversely affect financial results. The company uses hedging strategies to mitigate some of these risks, but they are not foolproof and carry counterparty risk.

SEC Filing: JETBLUE AIRWAYS CORP [ JBLU ] - 10-K - Feb. 12, 2026

data.title: BAXTER INTERNATIONAL INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Baxter International Inc., a global leader in medical products and therapies, has released its 2025 10-K report, detailing significant financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges faced over the past year. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's efforts to streamline operations, enhance product offerings, and navigate a complex market environment.

Financial Highlights

Total Net Sales: $11,244 million, an increase of 6% over 2024 on a reported basis and 3% on an operational sales basis.

Gross Margin: $3,379 million, representing 30.1% of net sales, a decrease from 37.5% in 2024, primarily due to the impact of the Kidney Care MSA and unfavorable manufacturing variances.

Total Operating Income (Loss): $(308) million, compared to $14 million in 2024, impacted by goodwill impairments and special items.

Net Income (Loss) Attributable to Baxter Stockholders: $(957) million, including special items which adversely impacted net income by $2.09 billion.

Diluted EPS: $(1.87) per share, reflecting the impact of special items and restructuring costs.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Baxter International Inc. manages its global operations based on three reportable segments: Medical Products & Therapies, Healthcare Systems & Technologies, and Pharmaceuticals. The Medical Products & Therapies segment includes sterile IV solutions, infusion systems, and surgical hemostat products. Healthcare Systems & Technologies covers smart bed systems and patient monitoring technologies. Pharmaceuticals include specialty injectable pharmaceuticals and inhaled anesthetics.

Geographical Performance: International sales totaled $5.12 billion in 2025, marking a 7% increase compared to 2024. Sales in the United States reached $6.12 billion, a 5% increase from the previous year. Emerging markets, including Eastern Europe, the Middle East, Africa, Latin America, and Asia (except Japan), saw a 3% increase in sales.

Sales Units: The Medical Products & Therapies segment saw a 2% increase in net sales, with Advanced Surgery products experiencing a 9% growth driven by increased sales volume globally. The Healthcare Systems & Technologies segment reported a 4% increase in net sales, with Care and Connectivity Solutions growing by 5% due to increased capital spending by customers.

New Product Launches: Baxter has been focusing on expanding its connected care portfolio, which includes smart bed systems, infusion pumps, and patient monitoring technologies. This is part of their innovation strategy to accelerate sales growth through new customer-centric offerings.

New Production Launches: Baxter continued to invest in capital expenditures, totaling $513 million in 2025, to support future growth and improve production efficiency. These investments are aimed at enhancing quality systems and optimizing manufacturing capabilities.

Future Outlook: Baxter is committed to driving sustainable growth and innovation, focusing on improving performance and fostering a culture of continuous improvement. The company plans to continue optimizing its portfolio and operational model to better align with commercial activities and respond effectively to market changes.

Strategic Initiatives

Baxter International Inc. undertook significant strategic initiatives, including the sale of its Kidney Care business to Carlyle Group Inc. for $3.80 billion, which was completed on January 31, 2025. This move was part of a broader strategy to streamline operations and focus on core business segments. Additionally, the company implemented a new operating model in the third quarter of 2023 to simplify operations and better align manufacturing and supply chain activities with commercial operations. The company also completed the sale of its BioPharma Solutions business in September 2023, receiving $3.96 billion in cash proceeds.

In 2025, Baxter repaid $3.81 billion of legacy indebtedness, primarily using the net after-tax cash proceeds from the sale of its Kidney Care business. The company also issued $2.00 billion in new notes to refinance existing debt. Baxter maintained a multicurrency revolving credit facility with a maximum capacity of $2.20 billion, maturing in 2030, and had no borrowings outstanding under this facility as of December 31, 2025. The company paid $348 million in cash dividends to stockholders in 2025 and did not repurchase any shares under its existing share repurchase program, which had $1.30 billion remaining available as of December 31, 2025.

Future Outlook: Baxter plans to continue optimizing its capital structure by reducing debt levels and exploring opportunities for capital investment to support growth. The company intends to pursue cost savings initiatives to mitigate dis-synergies from the sale of its Kidney Care business and further optimize its business and manufacturing footprint. Baxter's credit ratings were revised to BBB- by Standard & Poor's and Baa3 by Moody's, both with a stable outlook, reflecting the company's ongoing efforts to manage its financial position and leverage.

Challenges and Risks

The company faces significant risks related to its strategic actions, including the divestiture of its Kidney Care business and the acquisition of Hillrom. These actions have led to organizational changes and market exits, increasing vulnerability to market conditions and financial volatility. The company has yet to achieve its net leverage targets, constraining cash resources and growth initiatives.

The company is burdened by substantial indebtedness, requiring significant cash flow for debt service, which limits investments in R&D and other growth strategies. Failure to meet net leverage targets by 2026 could lead to credit rating downgrades, increasing borrowing costs and reducing capital access.

There is intense competition in the product markets, with risks of declining demand and pricing pressures. Competitors may respond more quickly to technological changes, potentially leading to loss of market share and reduced profitability.

The company has experienced supply chain disruptions, including production delays, increased costs, and shortages of raw materials. These challenges, exacerbated by global economic conditions and inflation, have negatively impacted sales and may continue to do so.

Management transitions have created uncertainties, with potential difficulties in attracting and retaining key employees, which could impact the company's ability to achieve financial and strategic objectives.

Management acknowledges the challenges posed by global economic conditions, including inflation and currency fluctuations, which have adversely affected operations. The company is focused on achieving operating efficiencies and sustaining cost reductions but recognizes the risks associated with restructuring initiatives.

The company is exposed to risks from incorporating AI and emerging technologies into its products and operations. There are uncertainties related to regulatory approvals, safety, and ethical implications, which could impact competitiveness and business prospects.

The company is subject to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which have affected the reported value of assets and liabilities. Interest rate changes and access to capital markets are also significant risks, with potential impacts on financial results and liquidity.

Overall, the company is navigating a complex landscape of operational, market, and regulatory risks, with strategic initiatives aimed at mitigating these challenges and positioning for future growth.

SEC Filing: BAXTER INTERNATIONAL INC [ BAX ] - 10-K - Feb. 12, 2026

data.title: Dover Declares Regular Quarterly Cash Dividend
data.description:
DOWNERS GROVE, Ill., Feb. 12, 2026 /PRNewswire/ — The Board of Directors of Dover Corporation today declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.52 (fifty-two cents) per share, payable on March 13, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 27, 2026.

About Dover:

Dover is a diversified global manufacturer and solutions provider with annual revenue of over $8 billion. We deliver innovative equipment and components, consumable supplies, aftermarket parts, software and digital solutions, and support services through five operating segments: Engineered Products, Clean Energy & Fueling, Imaging & Identification, Pumps & Process Solutions and Climate & Sustainability Technologies. Dover combines global scale with operational agility to lead the markets we serve. Recognized for our entrepreneurial approach for over 70 years, our team of approximately 24,000 employees takes an ownership mindset, collaborating with customers to redefine what's possible. Headquartered in Downers Grove, Illinois, Dover trades on the New York Stock Exchange under "DOV." Additional information is available at dovercorporation.com.

 
Investor Contact:                     Media Contact: 
Jack Dickens                          Adrian Sakowicz 
Vice President -- Investor Relations  Vice President -- Communications 
(630) 743-2566                        (630) 743-5039 
jdickens@dovercorp.com                asakowicz@dovercorp.com

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/dover-declares-regular-quarterly-cash-dividend-302686966.html

SOURCE Dover

data.title: AMP Illustrates How Turnaround Opportunities Carry Risk — Market Talk
data.description: Wealth manager AMP serves as a reminder that turnaround opportunities carry risk and rarely progress in a straight line, says Jefferies. AMP only missed Jefferies's forecast for FY 2025 underlying net profit by A$2 million. But analyst Simon Fitzgerald was disappointed by the overall composition of the result. "Underlying net profit in both the Platforms and AMP Bank segments fell short of our predictions by 7% and 13%, respectively," Jefferies says. "Additionally, FY26 indicates further revenue-margin declines in the wealth businesses, along with continued challenges for the bank." Jefferies cuts its price target by 20% to A$1.75/share, while retaining a buy call on the stock. AMP ended Thursday at A$1.28.(david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)
